Question: a) Explain why a goodness-of-fit test and a test of independence are generally right-tailed tests. b) If you did a left-tailed test, what would you be testing?
Answer: a) The test statistic is always positive and if the expected and observed values are not close together, the test statistic is large and the null hypothesis will be rejected. b) Testing to see if the data fits the distribution “too well” or is too perfect.
